audit_log.check: Autonomy vs Automation: PASS (intelligent campaign structure decisions based on spend level, not fixed templates)
Three-Layer Architecture: PASS (directive/orchestration/execution separated via M3 lanes)
Consciousness Awareness: PASS (maps to Neuro-Box 6D: SMART structure + SAFE budget management)
Quality Standards: PASS (learning phase minimums enforced, data consolidation required)
No Manipulation: PASS (honest attribution via ncROAS, no fake scarcity in budget tactics)
Proof Discipline: PASS (incremental attribution required, platform ROAS validated)
Platform Compliance: PASS (Meta Advertising Policies adherence, exclusion logic integrity)

dependency.integration_points: Campaign architecture houses creative concepts, testing frameworks determine creative volume requirements
dependency.notes: M3 lanes require creative diversity (Prospecting needs fresh concepts weekly, Retargeting needs direct response)

dependency.integration_points: ncROAS calculation validates M3 separation effectiveness, attribution windows configured per campaign
dependency.notes: Campaign structure enables clean attribution (Prospecting excludes purchasers for accurate ncROAS)

dependency.integration_points: Campaign architecture supports 3-phase scaling (Validation uses 1 CBO, Growth adds 2nd campaign, Scale uses 3-5 campaigns)
dependency.notes: Budget allocation formulas scale with spend levels (Cost Caps at $30k+/month)

consciousness_profile.description: Provides structural foundation for Meta ads campaigns using M3 Swim Lane Architecture to separate New Customer acquisition from Retargeting and Retention, preventing ROAS inflation and enabling clean attribution.

component.activation_strategy: RIGHT logical structure (separate lanes prevent data pollution) + BOTTOM risk management (exclusions protect budget allocation)
component.consciousness_purpose: Media buyers gain clarity (separate lanes = clean attribution) and safety (exclusions prevent wasted spend on existing customers)

balance_rules.rule: RIGHT_SMART before BOTTOM_SAFE: Understand lane logic before implementing exclusions
Exclusion integrity is BOTTOM_SAFE requirement (no overlap between Prospecting/Retargeting/Retention)
Budget allocation formulas are RIGHT_SMART (data-driven, not guesses)

component.activation_strategy: RIGHT logical testing (3:2:2 vs 5x5 based on data needs) + LEFT creative diversity (concepts not variations)
component.consciousness_purpose: Strategists understand when to use each framework (3:2:2 for stability, 5x5 for high variance) and how to ensure concept diversity (Andromeda requirement)

balance_rules.rule: RIGHT_LEFT balance: Testing logic (which framework) + Creative diversity (what to test)
Concept diversity is LEFT_SPECIAL requirement (test different stories, not color swaps)
Data consolidation is RIGHT_SMART requirement (group creatives to exit learning phase)

component.activation_strategy: BOTTOM risk management (learning phase minimums, scaling increments) + RIGHT logical scaling (20% max per adjustment, Cost Caps at $30k+)
component.consciousness_purpose: Campaign managers protect budget (don't scale too fast, don't fragment data) while achieving growth (systematic scaling protocol)

balance_rules.rule: BOTTOM_SAFE before scaling: Hit 50 conversions/week minimum before increasing budget
RIGHT_SMART scaling: 20% max budget increase per adjustment (avoid algorithm shock)
Cost Caps are BOTTOM_SAFE + RIGHT_SMART (limit risk on bad days, scale on good days)

core_paradigm.name: Andromeda: Creative IS Targeting
core_paradigm.source: [SRC: Dr. Matt Shiver | 53, THE ECOM KING | 373]
core_paradigm.principle: Meta's Andromeda algorithm matches users to creatives based on content analysis, not manual targeting inputs (interests, lookalikes, demographics). Broad targeting is the primary mechanism for scaling.

key_insights.insight: Manual targeting (Interests/Lookalikes) is largely obsolete or secondary [SRC: Dr. Matt Shiver | 53]
Restrictive targeting increases CPM because it fights Andromeda's optimization [SRC: THE ECOM KING | 373]
Creative diversity is critical — Andromeda penalizes repetitive formats [SRC: THE ECOM KING | 372]
Broad targeting works best when pixel has sufficient data (50+ conversions/week)
Exception: New pixels (fewer than 50 conversions) may benefit from Interest Clusters as temporary guide [SRC: Sam Piliero | 641]

implications_for_architecture.implication: Campaign structure must support broad targeting (remove demographic restrictions)
Ad sets should group creatives (3-6 per "Pack") to consolidate data, not fragment it
Testing frameworks (3:2:2, 5x5) must enforce concept diversity, not just variations
Exclusions focus on funnel stage (New vs Existing customers), not demographics

framework.name: M3 Swim Lane Architecture
framework.key_quote: "We need to make sure the distribution of spend is mostly focused on our new audiences."
framework.summary: A campaign structure that strictly separates New Customers (Prospecting), Engaged Audiences (Retargeting), and Existing Customers (Retention) to prevent ROAS inflation and enable clean attribution. This is the foundation structure for accounts spending >$3k/month where blended ROAS hides poor new customer acquisition.

when_to_use.trigger: Accounts spending >$3k/month where blended ROAS hides poor new customer acquisition
When retargeting ROAS above 4x but overall growth is stagnant (over-investing in existing customers)
When you need clean ncROAS measurement (new customer acquisition cost validation)

when_NOT_to_use.exclusion: Brand new accounts with fewer than 50 conversions/week (consolidate into 1-2 campaigns instead)
Very small budgets (under $1k/month) where fragmentation kills learning phase
When you don't have 180-day purchaser data (need historical conversion data for exclusions)

step.title: Create PROSPECTING CBO Campaign
step.action: Campaign objective: Sales (or Leads for lead gen)
Enable Campaign Budget Optimization (CBO)
Set daily budget based on learning phase minimum: (Target CPA × 50 conversions) / 7 days
Attribution: 7-day click or Incremental (avoid 1-day view over-attribution)

step.title: Configure PROSPECTING Exclusions
step.action: Create custom audience: "180-Day Purchasers" (all converters in last 180 days)
Create custom audience: "30-Day Site Visitors" (all site visitors last 30 days)
In Ad Set targeting: EXCLUDE both audiences (ensures only cold traffic)
step.rationale: Prospecting must target NEW customers only for accurate ncROAS calculation

step.title: Create RETARGETING CBO Campaign
step.action: Campaign objective: Sales (or Leads)
Enable CBO
Set budget: 20-30% of total ad spend (Prospecting gets 60-70%)

step.title: Configure RETARGETING Inclusions + Exclusions
step.action: INCLUDE: "30-Day Site Visitors" OR "Video Viewers 25%+" OR "Engagers" (Instagram/Facebook)
EXCLUDE: "180-Day Purchasers" (don't retarget existing customers, that's Retention's job)
step.rationale: Retargeting captures warm traffic who didn't buy yet, without wasting budget on existing customers

step.title: Create RETENTION CBO Campaign
step.action: Campaign objective: Sales
Enable CBO
Set budget: 5-10% of total ad spend (smallest lane)

step.title: Configure RETENTION Inclusion
step.action: INCLUDE: "180-Day Purchasers" (only target existing customers)
Creative: Win-back offers, upsells, cross-sells, loyalty rewards
step.rationale: Retention maximizes LTV from existing customers without inflating Prospecting ROAS

step.title: Validate Exclusion Logic
step.action: Verify no audience overlap between lanes using Audience Overlap tool in Ads Manager
Check: Prospecting excludes (Retargeting + Retention audiences)
Check: Retargeting excludes Retention audiences
step.circuit_breaker: IF overlap above 5% THEN fix exclusion logic before launching

key_metrics.metric: New customer revenue / ad spend (Prospecting lane only)
Total revenue / total ad spend (all channels)
Ensures growth focus on new customer acquisition
Supports conversion optimization without over-indexing on warm traffic
LTV optimization without inflating acquisition metrics

failure_mode.name: Exclusion Overlap Failure
failure_mode.symptoms: Prospecting ROAS inflated (>5x), but ncROAS low (below 1.5x); Retargeting audience showing "cold" behavior
failure_mode.root_cause: Exclusion logic not working — Prospecting is retargeting hot leads, or Retargeting is targeting purchasers
failure_mode.diagnosis: Check Audience Overlap tool; verify custom audience refresh rates (should update daily)
failure_mode.recovery: Fix exclusion logic, pause campaigns until verified, restart with clean audiences
failure_mode.prevention: Set custom audiences to auto-refresh daily; validate exclusions in Audience Overlap tool pre-launch

failure_mode.name: Over-Investment in Retargeting
failure_mode.symptoms: Retargeting ROAS above 4x, Prospecting ROAS below 2x, overall growth stagnant
failure_mode.root_cause: Budget shifted too heavily to Retargeting (easy wins), starving Prospecting (hard work)
failure_mode.diagnosis: Check budget distribution: IF Retargeting above 40% of total THEN over-indexed
failure_mode.recovery: Manually cap Retargeting budget at 30% max, force Prospecting budget increase by 20%
failure_mode.prevention: Set CBO budget limits per campaign (Prospecting minimum 60%, Retargeting maximum 30%)

golden_run.scenario: Ecom brand spending $10k/month, blended ROAS 3.5x but ncROAS unknown, wants to validate new customer profitability

inputs.goal: Separate M3 lanes to measure ncROAS accurately

execution.action: Create Prospecting CBO: $7k/month budget, exclude 180d purchasers + 30d visitors
Create Retargeting CBO: $2.5k/month budget, include 30d visitors, exclude 180d purchasers
Create Retention CBO: $500/month budget, include 180d purchasers
Launch all 3 campaigns simultaneously to avoid attribution drift
Monitor for 14 days to establish baseline ncROAS

expected_outputs.output: Prospecting ncROAS measured (target above 1.5x)
Retargeting ROAS isolated (typically 3-5x)
Retention ROAS isolated (typically 5-10x on win-back offers)
Clean attribution: No overlap, each lane shows true performance

success_criteria.criterion: ncROAS above 1.5x in Prospecting (sustainable for scaling)
Retargeting ROAS 3-5x (healthy warm traffic conversion)
Audience Overlap tool shows under 5% overlap between lanes
MER stable or improving (validates attribution cleanliness)

framework.name: The 3:2:2 Flexible Ad System
framework.key_quote: "Three creatives, two headlines, two primary texts... We are not trying to give everything a fair chance."
framework.summary: A specific ad-level testing structure designed to let Meta's AI determine the best combination of assets without fragmenting data across multiple ads. Uses Flexible Ad format to group 3 creatives × 2 headlines × 2 texts, allowing algorithm to test 12 combinations within a single ad (data consolidation for faster learning).

when_to_use.trigger: Testing new creative concepts within a broad audience (need data consolidation)
Accounts spending under $10k/month where data fragmentation is a risk
When launching a new offer or avatar (need stable testing environment)
When you have 3 distinct creative concepts ready to test (not variations of same concept)

when_NOT_to_use.exclusion: High-volume testing environments with above 10 concepts to test (use 5x5 instead)
When you need granular control over individual ad performance (use separate ads per concept)
When mixing formats (image + video) — Flexible Ads perform best with consistent format types

step.title: Select Flexible Ad Format
step.action: In Ads Manager, create new ad
Choose "Create Ad" , then Select "Flexible Ad" (not Manual Upload)
step.note: Flexible Ads allow Meta to auto-generate combinations of assets

step.title: Upload 3 Distinct Visual Creatives
step.action: Select format type: ALL video OR ALL image (do not mix)
Upload creative 1: Concept A (e.g., Founder-led VSL)
Upload creative 2: Concept B (e.g., UGC testimonial)
Upload creative 3: Concept C (e.g., Product demo)
step.quality_gate: Ensure creatives are CONCEPTS (different hooks/stories), not VARIATIONS (same hook, different colors)

step.title: Write 2 Distinct Primary Texts
step.action: Primary Text 1: Long-form story or educational (100-150 words)
Primary Text 2: Short-form benefit-driven or curiosity (30-50 words)
step.quality_gate: Texts should test different angles (story vs feature, long vs short)

step.title: Write 2 Distinct Headlines
step.action: Headline 1: Promise-based (e.g., "Get [Outcome] in [Timeframe]")
Headline 2: Objection-based (e.g., "Even If [Obstacle], This Works")
step.quality_gate: Headlines should complement primary texts (test different persuasion approaches)

step.title: Launch in Broad Ad Set
step.action: Place Flexible Ad in Prospecting campaign's Broad ad set
Let run for minimum 7 days or 50 conversions (whichever comes first)
step.monitoring: Check "Spend Share" metric to see which asset combinations get budget allocation

step.title: Analyze Asset Performance
step.action: After 7 days, go to "View Asset Performance" in ad breakdown
Identify winning creative (highest spend share + lowest CPA)
Identify winning headline + text combo
step.decision: IF clear winner emerges (30%+ better CPA) THEN harvest Post ID to scaling campaign
IF no clear winner (all within 10%) THEN test was too similar (variations not concepts), restart with more diverse concepts

metric.name: Spend Share %
metric.threshold: Winner should get above 40% of budget

key_metrics.metric: Percentage of ad budget allocated to each asset combination (Meta's signal of predicted performance)

metric.name: CPA by Asset
metric.threshold: Winner should be 30%+ better than median

key_metrics.metric: Cost per acquisition for each creative/headline/text combination
(Unique hooks + unique mechanisms) / total asset count — detects if testing concepts vs variations

failure_mode.name: Format Mixing Confusion
failure_mode.symptoms: All creatives get equal spend share (~33% each), no clear winner emerges, CPA volatile
failure_mode.root_cause: Mixed formats (image + video) in one Flexible Ad confuses algorithm's optimization
failure_mode.diagnosis: Check if creatives are consistent format (all video OR all image)
failure_mode.recovery: Separate into 2 Flexible Ads: one for video concepts, one for image concepts
failure_mode.prevention: Never mix video and image in same Flexible Ad — keep format types consistent

failure_mode.name: Variation Testing Trap
failure_mode.symptoms: All asset combinations perform within 10% of each other, no statistical winner
failure_mode.root_cause: Testing variations (same concept, minor differences) instead of distinct concepts
failure_mode.diagnosis: Review creatives: Are hooks truly different, or just visual tweaks of same story?
failure_mode.recovery: Kill test, create 3 NEW concepts with different hooks/stories/mechanisms
failure_mode.prevention: Use concept diversity validation: Each creative must have unique hook, unique mechanism, or unique proof type

golden_run.scenario: SaaS company testing 3 creative concepts for cold traffic, need to identify winner fast

inputs.goal: Identify winning concept within 7 days for scaling

execution.action: Create Flexible Ad with 3 video creatives (all video format)
Write 2 primary texts: (1) Long-form problem-solution story, (2) Short-form benefit bullets
Write 2 headlines: (1) "10X Your Workflow in 30 Days", (2) "Even If You're Not Tech-Savvy"
Launch in Prospecting Broad ad set with $285/day budget
Monitor daily: Check Spend Share to see which combo gets budget

expected_outputs.output: By Day 3-4: Clear spend share leader emerges (e.g., Dashboard demo gets 55% of budget)
By Day 7: Winner identified with 30%+ lower CPA than other combos
Winning Post ID harvested to scaling campaign
Losing concepts paused or iterated with new hooks

success_criteria.criterion: Clear winner emerges (>40% spend share, 30%+ better CPA)
Learning phase exits under 7 days (enough conversion volume)
Winner CPA within target (e.g., under $100 for SaaS trial)

framework.name: The Pack System (CBO Scaling)
framework.key_quote: "Every time you have a new creative... that gets grouped together in a new pack."
framework.summary: Launching new creative batches as dated Ad Sets within an existing Prospecting CBO campaign. This organizational method allows for high-volume testing without resetting the learning phase of existing winners. CBO automatically reallocates budget to best-performing packs while allowing continuous testing of new concepts.

when_to_use.trigger: High-volume creative testing environments (shipping 5-10 concepts weekly)
Scaling accounts spending >$20k/month where creative volume is the bottleneck
When you need organized tracking of creative batches by launch date

when_NOT_to_use.exclusion: Brand new accounts with fewer than 3 creatives total (consolidate into one ad set instead)
Low-budget accounts under $3k/month (data fragmentation risk)
When testing entirely different avatars (use separate campaigns instead)

step.title: Create Prospecting CBO Campaign (One Time)
step.action: Campaign objective: Sales (or Leads)
Enable CBO with daily budget matching learning phase minimum
Exclusions: 180-day Purchasers + 30-day Site Visitors

step.title: Launch First Pack
step.action: Create Ad Set: "Broad Pack 2026-01-19"
Targeting: Broad (Age 18-65+, All Genders, Geographic limit only)
Upload 3-6 new creative concepts (not variations)

step.title: Monitor for 7 Days
step.action: Let pack run minimum 7 days or 50 conversions (whichever first)
Check Ad Set-level metrics: CPA, Spend Share, ROAS

step.title: Launch Subsequent Packs Weekly
step.action: Following week: Create new Ad Set "Broad Pack 2026-01-26"
Load next batch of 3-6 new concepts
CBO will automatically shift budget to best-performing packs

step.title: Manage Pack Performance
step.action: Do NOT pause old packs prematurely (let CBO starve them naturally)
Only pause packs with Frequency above 4.0 + rising CPA for 3+ days
step.circuit_breaker: IF a pack gets zero spend for 5 days THEN it lost; pause it

metric.name: Pack Spend Distribution
metric.threshold: No single pack above 70% of total

key_metrics.metric: Healthy distribution means algorithm finding winners across multiple concepts

metric.name: Pack CPA
metric.threshold: Best pack under 20% better than median

key_metrics.metric: Indicates whether new concepts are competitive or need improvement
Measures creative shelf life; informs production cadence

failure_mode.name: Pack Starvation
failure_mode.symptoms: New packs get $0 spend for 3+ days; one old pack hogs 95% of budget
failure_mode.root_cause: CBO's predictive model strongly favors historical winner over unknown new concepts
failure_mode.diagnosis: Check spend distribution across ad sets in last 3 days
failure_mode.recovery: Apply "Ad Set Spending Limits" (Min = 1x Target CPA) to starved pack for 7 days
failure_mode.prevention: Launch packs with Ad Set Minimum enabled from day 1; remove after learning phase

failure_mode.name: Premature Pack Killing
failure_mode.symptoms: Pausing packs after 2-3 days of low performance, never finding winners
failure_mode.root_cause: Reactionary pausing before algorithm completes learning phase
failure_mode.diagnosis: Check: Did pack exit learning phase? (Needs 50 conversions or 7 days minimum)
failure_mode.recovery: Restore paused packs, let run full 7 days before evaluation
failure_mode.prevention: Set calendar reminders for "Day 7 Pack Review" instead of daily micro-management

golden_run.scenario: Ecom brand scaling from $10k to $50k/month, needs organized creative testing structure

inputs.creative_capacity: 5-10 new concepts per week from creative team
inputs.goal: Systematically test new concepts without disrupting existing winners

execution.action: Week 1: Launch Prospecting CBO with "Pack Jan-19" (6 concepts)
Week 2: Add "Pack Jan-26" (5 concepts) as new ad set in same CBO
Week 3: Add "Pack Feb-02" (8 concepts); CBO now managing 3 packs
Monitor: Pack Jan-19 gets 60% spend (winner), Jan-26 gets 30%, Feb-02 gets 10% (learning)
Week 4: Jan-19 fatigue (Frequency 4.2) detected; Feb-02 emerges as new winner taking 50% spend

expected_outputs.output: CBO naturally graduates winners and starves losers without manual intervention
Clear tracking of which creative batch (by date) is driving performance
Ability to scale to 15-20 active concepts across 4-5 packs without fragmentation

success_criteria.criterion: No single pack dominates above 80% of spend (indicates healthy diversity)
New packs able to compete within 5-7 days (learning phase exits)
Winning packs maintain performance 14+ days before refresh needed

framework.name: Post ID Harvesting
framework.key_quote: "You've isolated the unicorn... and you've added it to your all-star team."
framework.summary: Moving a winning ad from a testing environment (Flexible Ads, dynamic creative) to a scaling environment (Control/Scale campaign) by copying its Post ID rather than duplicating the ad. This preserves all social proof (likes, comments, shares) and engagement history, resulting in lower CPM and higher conversion rates when scaled.

when_to_use.trigger: Test ad shows high engagement (100+ likes/comments) AND profitable CPA
Ready to scale winning concept without losing social momentum
When ad has strong organic reach (shares increasing daily)

when_NOT_to_use.exclusion: Ad has negative comments or complaint threads (harvest clean posts only)
Test still in learning phase (fewer than 50 conversions, under 7 days runtime)
When Post ID is from competitor's page (can only harvest your own posts)

step.title: Identify Winner in Test Campaign
step.action: Review Flexible Ad or Test ad set performance
Filter by: CPA below Target AND Spend above $500 (statistical significance)
Visual check: Ad has likes/comments/shares (social proof present)

step.title: Locate the Post ID
step.action: In Ads Manager, go to Ads tab
Find the winning ad
Click "Edit" , then Preview
In preview, click "Facebook Posts with Comments"
Copy the Post URL from browser (contains Post ID in URL)

step.title: Create New Ad in Scaling Campaign Using Post ID
step.action: Navigate to your Scaling/Control campaign
Create new ad , then Select "Use Existing Post"
Paste Post ID or URL
Meta will pull the exact post with all engagement intact

step.title: Launch and Monitor
step.action: Keep original test ad running (do not pause immediately)
Monitor both for 48 hours
Compare CPMs: Harvested post should show 10-30% lower CPM due to social proof

step.title: Scale the Harvested Winner
step.action: Once harvested ad proves stable (matching or beating test performance)
Increase Scaling campaign budget by 20%
Can now pause original test ad (engagement is preserved on harvested version)

key_metrics.metric: Validates harvesting worked correctly (should see identical engagement count)

metric.name: CPM Improvement
metric.threshold: 10-30% lower CPM on harvested vs duplicate

key_metrics.metric: Measures efficiency gain from social proof
Confirms performance translates from test to scale environment

failure_mode.name: Lost Engagement
failure_mode.symptoms: Harvested ad shows zero likes/comments; appears as new post
failure_mode.root_cause: Incorrect Post ID copied, or used "Duplicate Ad" instead of "Use Existing Post"
failure_mode.diagnosis: Check: Did you select "Use Existing Post" option? Verify Post ID in URL matches original
failure_mode.recovery: Delete incorrect ad, re-harvest using correct Post ID workflow
failure_mode.prevention: Always verify engagement count matches immediately after creating harvested ad

failure_mode.name: Performance Degradation After Harvest
failure_mode.symptoms: Harvested ad CPA 50%+ worse than original test ad
failure_mode.root_cause: Different audience targeting or placement settings in scaling campaign vs test
failure_mode.diagnosis: Compare targeting/placements between test and scale campaigns (should be identical)
failure_mode.recovery: Match scaling campaign settings to exact test campaign configuration
failure_mode.prevention: Use campaign templates; document test campaign settings before harvesting

golden_run.scenario: SaaS company finds winning Flexible Ad in test campaign, wants to scale without losing engagement

inputs.goal: Scale to $10k/day spend while preserving social proof

execution.action: Identify winner: Founder VSL video in Flexible Ad (got 65% of spend share)
Copy Post ID from Facebook post preview
Create new ad in "Scaling CBO" using "Use Existing Post" , then paste Post ID
Verify: New ad shows 350 likes, 42 comments (engagement transferred)
Launch with $1k/day budget in scaling campaign

expected_outputs.output: Harvested ad CPM: $32 (vs $45 CPM on fresh duplicate)
CPA maintained: $48 (within 10% of original $45)
ROAS: 3.0x (slight drop acceptable due to scale)
Engagement continues growing: 500+ likes within 3 days

success_criteria.criterion: Social proof fully transferred (engagement count matches)
CPM reduction of 10-30% vs creating new duplicate ad
CPA within 20% of test performance
Able to scale to 5x test budget without performance collapse

framework.name: Ad Set Spending Limits (Forced Testing)
framework.key_quote: "Set your daily minimum to one time your target cost per acquisition."
framework.summary: Using Min/Max daily spend limits at the Ad Set level within a CBO campaign to force the algorithm to allocate budget to new creative packs that would otherwise receive zero spend. This is a tactical intervention when CBO's predictive model over-indexes on historical winners and starves new tests before they can prove themselves.

when_to_use.trigger: Launching new creative packs in established CBOs where one ad set hogs 90%+ of budget
When new test ad sets get $0 spend for 3+ consecutive days
Forcing algorithm to give new concepts a fair trial period

when_NOT_to_use.exclusion: Ad set already receiving above 20% of CBO budget (it's getting a fair trial)
When total CBO budget less than 3x the minimum limit you'd set (causes budget fragmentation)
Leaving limits on indefinitely for poor-performing ad sets (defeats purpose)

step.title: Identify Starved Ad Set
step.action: Review CBO spend distribution over last 3 days
Find new ad set receiving under 5% of budget or $0
Confirm it's not paused and has active creatives

step.title: Calculate Minimum Spend Requirement
step.action: Determine your Target CPA (e.g., $50)
Set Daily Minimum = 1x Target CPA ($50/day)
step.rationale: This ensures at least 1 conversion per day for learning phase data

step.title: Apply Ad Set Spending Limit
step.action: Go to starved ad set, then Budget and Schedule section
Enable "Ad set spending limit"
Enter Daily Minimum: $50 (or your calculated amount)
Optional: Set Daily Maximum if you want to cap risk (e.g., $200)

step.title: Set Calendar Reminder for 7-Day Review
step.action: Mark calendar for exactly 7 days from launch
This is your evaluation checkpoint (gives learning phase time)

step.title: Day 7 Decision
step.action: Review ad set performance over full 7 days
step.decision: IF CPA below Target AND spend increased naturally, Remove limit (it's a winner, let it fly)
IF CPA above Target AND spend stayed at minimum, Pause ad set (it failed, no point forcing it)
IF inconclusive (borderline performance) , then Extend limit for another 3 days max

metric.name: Spend Liberation
metric.threshold: Ad set should exceed minimum by Day 5-7

key_metrics.metric: If spend naturally rises above minimum, algorithm sees potential
Primary success indicator; if not achieved, test failed

metric.name: Learning Phase Exit
metric.threshold: Should exit by Day 7 with minimum spend

key_metrics.metric: Confirms sufficient conversion volume

failure_mode.name: Permanent Limit Trap
failure_mode.symptoms: Ad set spending exactly the minimum every day for 14+ days; never scales
failure_mode.root_cause: Limit left on indefinitely for a losing ad set; CBO forced to waste budget
failure_mode.diagnosis: Check: Is CPA consistently above Target? Is spend locked at exact minimum daily?
failure_mode.recovery: Pause ad set immediately; wasted budget could have gone to winners
failure_mode.prevention: Always set Day 7 calendar reminder to review and remove/pause

failure_mode.name: Budget Fragmentation
failure_mode.symptoms: CBO campaign spending fragmented across 5+ ad sets with limits; overall CPA rising
failure_mode.root_cause: Too many limits set simultaneously, preventing CBO optimization
failure_mode.diagnosis: Count active ad sets with limits; if above 2 simultaneously, you're over-constraining
failure_mode.recovery: Remove limits from all but the 1-2 newest tests; let CBO breathe
failure_mode.prevention: Maximum 1-2 ad sets with limits at any time in a single CBO

golden_run.scenario: Established CBO with one dominant "Spend Hog" ad set; new test gets zero budget for 4 days

execution.action: Day 1: New Pack Jan-19 launched, receives $0 spend (CBO favoring Jan-01 pack)
Day 4: Still $0; apply Ad Set Spending Limit
Set Daily Minimum: $50 (1x Target CPA)
Day 5-7: Pack Jan-19 now forced to spend $50/day minimum
Monitor: CPA trends, spend share changes

expected_outputs.output: Day 5: Pack Jan-19 spends $50, generates 1 conversion, CPA $50 (at target)
Day 6: Algorithm sees potential; spend increases to $75 naturally, CPA drops to $45
Day 7: Spend reaches $120, CPA stable at $46; limit can be removed
Day 10: Pack Jan-19 now capturing 40% of CBO budget organically; new winner emerged

success_criteria.criterion: New ad set receives forced minimum spend ($50/day)
CPA below Target by Day 7 ($50 or better)
Spend naturally exceeds minimum by Day 6-7 (algorithm recognizes potential)
After limit removed, ad set continues receiving budget (sustainable winner)

framework.name: 5x5 Testing Matrix
framework.key_quote: "Five unique ad copy... five unique headlines... five unique creatives."
framework.summary: A high-volume testing structure designed to feed Meta's algorithm maximum creative variety in a single ad set. The matrix generates up to 125 potential combinations (5 creatives × 5 primary texts × 5 headlines) using Flexible Ad format, allowing the algorithm to rapidly surface winning combinations. Best for initial account launches, quarterly resets, or high-budget accounts with creative capacity.

when_to_use.trigger: Initial account launch (finding product-market fit, testing multiple angles)
Quarterly creative resets when performance has plateaued
Scaling accounts spending $30k+/month with creative production capacity for 25 assets
When you need rapid learning (7-10 days to identify winning patterns)

when_NOT_to_use.exclusion: Small budgets under $5k/month (insufficient spend to test 125 combinations)
Limited creative resources (can't produce 5 distinct creatives + 10 copy variations)
When you already have proven winners (use 3:2:2 to test variations instead)

step.title: Create 5 Distinct Visual Creatives
step.action: Creative 1: Video - Founder/Authority (Face to camera, story-driven)
Creative 2: Video - UGC/Testimonial (Customer review, authentic)
Creative 3: Video - Product Demo (How it works, benefits)
Creative 4: Static - Lifestyle Image (Product in use, aspirational)
Creative 5: Static - Lazy Static (Product shot + text overlay)
step.quality_gate: Ensure formats are MEANINGFULLY different (test concepts, not button colors)

step.title: Write 5 Distinct Primary Texts
step.action: Text 1: Long-form Story (150+ words, narrative-driven, founder journey)
Text 2: Short-form Benefits (50 words, bullet points, feature list)
Text 3: Social Proof (100 words, testimonial aggregation, stars/reviews)
Text 4: Problem-Agitate (75 words, pain point focus, "Before" state)
Text 5: Educational/How-To (125 words, instructional, value-driven)
step.quality_gate: Each text should test different persuasion approach (emotion vs logic vs authority)

step.title: Write 5 Distinct Headlines
step.action: Headline 1: Promise-Based ("Get [Outcome] in [Timeframe]")
Headline 2: Objection-Handling ("Even If [Obstacle], This Works")
Headline 3: Curiosity-Driven ("The [Category] Secret Nobody Tells You")
Headline 4: Direct Offer ("[X]% Off [Product] - Today Only")
Headline 5: Social Proof ("[X] Customers Can't Be Wrong")

step.title: Launch as Single Flexible Ad
step.action: Create new ad in Prospecting Broad ad set
Select "Flexible Ad" format (allows Meta to mix and match assets)
Upload all 5 creatives, 5 primary texts, 5 headlines
Launch with budget sufficient for learning phase: (Target CPA × 50 conversions) / 7 days

step.title: Monitor Spend Share and Asset Performance
step.action: Day 3: Check "View Asset Performance" to see early leaders
Day 5-7: Clear patterns should emerge (certain creatives getting 40%+ spend share)
Identify top 3 asset combinations by CPA and Spend Share

step.title: Graduate Winners and Iterate Losers
step.action: Harvest Post IDs of top 3 combinations to scaling campaign
For losing assets (getting under 5% spend share by Day 7): Replace with new concepts and retest

metric.name: Asset Spend Distribution
metric.threshold: Top 3 combos should capture 60%+ of spend by Day 7

key_metrics.metric: Indicates algorithm found strong preferences

metric.name: Combination Count Tested
metric.threshold: Meta should test 20+ combinations (out of 125 possible)

key_metrics.metric: Shows adequate variety in testing
Enough volume to make statistical decisions

failure_mode.name: Variation Trap
failure_mode.symptoms: All 5 creatives get equal spend share (~20% each); no clear winner by Day 10
failure_mode.root_cause: Testing variations (same concept, minor tweaks) instead of distinct concepts
failure_mode.diagnosis: Review creatives side-by-side: Are they 80% similar? (Same hook, different color = variation)
failure_mode.recovery: Kill test; create 5 NEW concepts with entirely different hooks, stories, mechanisms
failure_mode.prevention: Concept diversity validation pre-launch: Each creative must have unique hook OR unique mechanism OR unique proof type

failure_mode.name: Budget Starvation
failure_mode.symptoms: Algorithm cycling through combinations slowly; Day 10 still in learning phase
failure_mode.root_cause: Insufficient budget to test 125 combinations (need ~$3-5k minimum for 7-day test)
failure_mode.diagnosis: Check conversion volume: Are you hitting 50 conversions in 7 days? If no, budget too low
failure_mode.recovery: Either increase budget 2-3x OR reduce to 3:2:2 format (fewer combinations)
failure_mode.prevention: Use 5x5 only if spending $5k+ on test; otherwise use 3:2:2

golden_run.scenario: Info product launch, testing multiple angles to find product-market fit messaging

inputs.goal: Identify which creative angle + copy approach resonates for scaling

execution.action: Create 5 video creatives: (1) Founder Story, (2) Student Testimonial, (3) Whiteboard Lesson, (4) Results Screenshot, (5) Behind-the-Scenes
Write 5 primary texts: Long Story, Short Benefits, Social Proof, Problem-Agitate, How-To
Write 5 headlines: Promise, Objection, Curiosity, Offer, Social Proof
Launch as Flexible Ad in Prospecting Broad CBO
Monitor asset performance daily

expected_outputs.output: Day 3: Early leaders emerge (Student Testimonial creative + Social Proof text getting 45% spend)
Day 7: Top 3 combinations identified with CPAs of $18, $22, $24 (all under $25 target)
Losers identified: Whiteboard creative getting under 2% spend (boring for cold traffic)
Winning pattern discovered: Social proof messaging + authentic (non-polished) creatives work best

success_criteria.criterion: Clear winners emerge (3 combinations with 30%+ lower CPA than losers)
At least 1 combination hits under $25 target CPA with statistical significance (50+ conversions)
Actionable learnings identified (e.g., "Social proof copy beats long-form story for this audience")
Winners ready to scale via Post ID harvesting to dedicated scaling campaign

framework.name: Lead Gen Lag-Time Optimization
framework.key_quote: "If we wait all the way to the end point we have a massive gap in between."
framework.summary: Adjusting the optimization event based on sales cycle length to ensure the pixel receives conversion data within the 7-day attribution window. For businesses with long sales cycles (>7 days from lead to sale), optimizing for bottom-funnel events starves the algorithm. Instead, optimize for mid-funnel events (Qualified Lead, Booked Call) that occur within the attribution window, ensuring consistent pixel feedback for learning.

when_to_use.trigger: High-ticket B2B or service businesses with sales cycles above 7 days
Complex products requiring consultations/demos before purchase
When optimizing for Purchase yields zero conversion data in Ads Manager

when_NOT_to_use.exclusion: Ecom with instant checkout (optimize for Purchase directly)
Low-ticket offers with under 24 hour decision cycles
When you have above 50 Purchase conversions/week (sufficient data for bottom-funnel optimization)

step.title: Calculate Lag Time (Lead to Sale)
step.action: Pull CRM data for last 100 closed deals
Calculate: Date of Lead Capture , then Date of Sale
Determine median lag time (e.g., 14 days, 21 days, etc.)

step.title: Determine Optimization Event Based on Lag Time
step.decision: IF Lag Time under 7 days , then Optimize for Bottom-Funnel (Sale, Purchase, Closed Deal)
IF Lag Time 7-14 days , then Optimize for Mid-Funnel (Qualified Lead, Booked Appointment)
IF Lag Time above 14 days , then Optimize for Top-Funnel (Lead Submission, Content Download)
step.rationale: Must select event that fires within 7-day attribution window for consistent pixel feedback

step.title: Define and Implement Optimization Event
step.action: If mid-funnel: Create custom conversion event "Qualified Lead" (lead who answers key questions)
Or use "Schedule" event (booked call/demo via Calendly, etc.)
Implement tracking: Pixel fires when user completes qualification form or books appointment
Validate in Events Manager: Confirm 15-30 events/week minimum

step.title: Launch Campaign with Mid-Funnel Optimization
step.action: Campaign objective: Leads (or Conversions)
Optimization event: "Qualified Lead" or "Schedule"
Budget: (Target Cost-Per-Qualified-Lead × 50) / 7 days

step.title: Monitor Quality and Back-End Conversion Rate
step.action: Track: Qualified Lead , then Booked Call rate (should be above 30%)
Track: Booked Call , then Closed Deal rate (benchmark to historical)
step.circuit_breaker: IF Qualified Lead quality drops (show rate below 50%) THEN add friction to form (more questions)

metric.name: Lag Time (Median)
metric.threshold: Must be under 7 days for bottom-funnel optimization

key_metrics.metric: Determines which funnel stage to optimize for
Pixel needs sufficient data; if below, move higher in funnel

metric.name: Qualified Lead , then Sale Conversion Rate

key_metrics.metric: Validates lead quality hasn't degraded by optimizing higher in funnel

failure_mode.name: Pixel Malnutrition
failure_mode.symptoms: Zero conversions showing in Ads Manager despite leads/sales happening in CRM
failure_mode.root_cause: Optimizing for event that occurs outside 7-day attribution window (e.g., Sale with 21-day lag)
failure_mode.diagnosis: Check Events Manager: When was last Purchase event received? If above 7 days ago, lag time disconnect confirmed
failure_mode.recovery: Immediately switch optimization to higher-funnel event (Lead or Qualified Lead) that fires within 7 days
failure_mode.prevention: Always calculate lag time before campaign launch; select optimization event accordingly

failure_mode.name: Junk Lead Spiral
failure_mode.symptoms: Low cost per lead ($5-10) but zero backend conversions (calls booked, sales closed)
failure_mode.root_cause: Optimizing for top-funnel (Lead Submission) without quality filter; algorithm finding bots/low-intent users
failure_mode.diagnosis: Check lead quality: Are form submissions from real emails? Are leads answering qualifying questions?
failure_mode.recovery: Add friction to form (require phone, add qualifying question) OR optimize for "Booked Call" instead of "Lead"
failure_mode.prevention: For B2B/high-ticket, always optimize for Qualified Lead (with qualification criteria) not raw Lead volume

golden_run.scenario: B2B SaaS company selling $10k annual contracts with 18-day sales cycle (Lead , then Demo , then Sale)

inputs.problem: Optimizing for "Purchase" yields zero pixel data (18 days above 7-day window)

execution.action: Calculate lag time: Lead , then Sale = 18 days (median)
Decision: Must optimize for event within 7 days
Select optimization event: "Booked Demo" (occurs Day 5-7, within window)
Implement tracking: Calendly integration fires "Schedule" event when demo booked
Launch campaign optimizing for "Schedule" event
Budget: $1,500/day to generate 10 demos/day

expected_outputs.output: Pixel receives 50-70 "Schedule" events per week (sufficient for optimization)
Cost per booked demo: $145 (within $150 target)
Demo , then Sale conversion rate: 25% (matches historical)
Algorithm learns to find "demo-quality" leads, not just cheap form fills

success_criteria.criterion: Pixel receiving 15-30 optimization events per week minimum
Cost per event (Booked Demo) within target ($150 or less)
Backend conversion rate (Demo , then Sale) matches historical benchmarks (no quality degradation)
Campaign exits learning phase under 7 days (sufficient event volume)

framework.name: Value Rules Bidding
framework.key_quote: "Create rule sets... based on outcomes that Facebook can't see like lifetime value."
framework.summary: Automatically adjusting bids based on high-value customer attributes that the platform can't see in real-time (LTV, repeat purchase rate, geographic profitability). By creating Value Rules, you tell Meta to bid higher for segments you know are more profitable (e.g., Men have 2x LTV, so bid +20% for male audience) or lower for lower-value segments (international markets, lower-tier geographies). This optimizes for business profit, not just platform ROAS.

when_to_use.trigger: Advanced accounts with clear LTV data by demographic/geographic segments
Spending $30k+/month where bid precision impacts profitability
When certain customer segments have 30%+ higher LTV than others
Scaling internationally while maintaining blended efficiency

when_NOT_to_use.exclusion: No LTV data or backend analytics (need 6+ months of customer data minimum)
Small budgets under $10k/month (insufficient spend for segment optimization)
When all customer segments have similar LTV (no differentiation to leverage)

step.title: Analyze Backend LTV Data
step.action: Pull customer data from CRM/Analytics (minimum 6-12 months)
Segment by: Gender, Age, Location, Device, etc.
Calculate LTV by segment: Total revenue from segment / Number of customers
Identify high-value segments (30%+ higher LTV than average)

step.title: Define Value Rule Logic
step.action: Example: "Men have 150% LTV vs Women (M: $300, F: $200)"
Calculate bid adjustment: +25% bid for Men (to reflect higher value)
Or: "California customers have 80% LTV vs Texas (CA: $240, TX: $300)"
Calculate bid adjustment: -20% bid for California

step.title: Implement Value Rules in Campaign
step.action: Navigate to Campaign or Ad Set level , then "Value Optimization"
Click "Create Value Rules"
Rule 1: IF Gender = Male , then Increase Bid by 25%
Rule 2: IF Location = Tier 2 Countries (not US/UK) , then Decrease Bid by 15%
Save and activate

step.title: Monitor Segment Performance
step.action: After 14 days, break down results by segment
Check: Did high-bid segments maintain or improve ROAS?
Check: Did spend shift toward high-value segments as intended?

step.title: Iterate Rules Based on Results
step.action: If segment ROAS drops: Reduce bid adjustment (e.g., +25% , then +15%)
If segment still under-represented in spend: Increase bid adjustment further

metric.name: LTV by Segment
metric.threshold: High-value segment should be 30%+ higher LTV

key_metrics.metric: Justifies bid differentiation

metric.name: Spend Distribution Shift
metric.threshold: High-bid segments should increase spend share by 10-20%

key_metrics.metric: Indicates rules are working as intended

metric.name: Blended ROAS Maintenance
metric.threshold: Overall ROAS should hold or improve

key_metrics.metric: Validates that bidding more for high-LTV customers is profitable

failure_mode.name: Overfitted Rules
failure_mode.symptoms: Spend becomes 90% concentrated in one segment; overall volume drops
failure_mode.root_cause: Bid adjustment too aggressive (+50%+), causing algorithm to ignore other segments entirely
failure_mode.diagnosis: Check spend distribution: Is 1 segment getting above 70% of budget?
failure_mode.recovery: Reduce bid adjustment to +10-20% maximum; allow diversification
failure_mode.prevention: Start with conservative bid adjustments (+10-15%); increase gradually

failure_mode.name: Stat-Sig Trap
failure_mode.symptoms: Rules based on segments with fewer than 100 customers; performance erratic
failure_mode.root_cause: Insufficient sample size for LTV calculation; one whale customer skews data
failure_mode.diagnosis: Check: Does high-LTV segment have 100+ customers? If no, data not reliable
failure_mode.recovery: Remove rule; wait until segment reaches statistical significance (100+ customers)
failure_mode.prevention: Only create rules for segments with 100+ customers minimum

golden_run.scenario: Ecom subscription brand with 12 months of customer data, clear LTV differences by gender and geography

inputs.goal: Shift more spend to Men (higher LTV) without tanking overall ROAS

execution.action: Calculate: Men have 59% higher LTV than Women ($350 vs $220)
Create Value Rule: IF Gender = Male , then Increase Bid by 20%
Create Value Rule: IF Location NOT in (US) , then Decrease Bid by 10%
Launch rules in existing Prospecting CBO
Monitor for 14 days

expected_outputs.output: Spend distribution shifts: 75% Men, 25% Women (from 60/40)
CPA for Men increases slightly ($45 , then $50) but LTV justifies it
Blended ROAS improves: 2.8x , then 3.1x (more high-LTV customers acquired)
International spend decreases from 30% , then 20% (US prioritized)

success_criteria.criterion: Spend shifts toward high-LTV segment (Men) by 10-15%
Blended ROAS maintained or improved (validates LTV thesis)
Backend LTV per acquired customer increases (measure 30 days post-campaign)

sop.name: The Creative Flywheel (Weekly Routine)
sop.purpose: Systematically identify winning concepts and produce new creative variations to maintain performance and prevent fatigue.

step.title: Analyze Last 7-14 Days Performance
step.action: Open Ads Manager, filter by last 7-14 days
Switch attribution to "Incremental" (to see true lift, not retargeting)
Sort ads by Incremental ROAS descending

step.title: Identify Top Concepts (Not Just Individual Ads)
step.action: Look for patterns across top 5 performers
Group by concept type: UGC in car? Press screenshot? Founder story? Product demo?
Note: 1 concept may have 3-5 individual ad variations

step.title: Brief 4-6 Iterations of Winning Concept
step.action: Example: If "UGC in car" is winning concept:
Iterate 1: Different car (sedan vs SUV)
Iterate 2: Different person (gender/age variation)
Iterate 3: Different time of day (morning vs night)
Iterate 4-6: Combine variations

step.title: Source 1 New Concept from Competitors
step.action: Use MagicBrief, Foreplay, or Meta Ads Library
Filter: Ads running above 14 days (proven winners)
Find 1 concept you haven't tested (e.g., testimonial grid, timeline ad, comparison chart)
Brief creative team to produce your version

step.title: Launch New Creative Batch
step.action: Upload all new creatives (4-6 iterations + 1 new concept) to Prospecting CBO
Create new Ad Set: "Broad Pack [Date]" (Pack System)
Let CBO allocate budget; monitor for 7 days

quality_gates.gate: Winning concepts must have Incremental ROAS above 1.5x (not just platform ROAS)
New concept sourced from competitor must be running above 14 days (proven, not experimental)
Creative briefs must specify concept (not just "make a video") with visual references

sop.name: Campaign Launch Checklist (Pre-Launch)
sop.purpose: Ensure all campaigns launch with correct structure, targeting, exclusions, and creative setup to maximize learning phase efficiency.

step.title: Campaign Level Configuration
step.action: Objective: Sales (Ecom) or Leads (Lead Gen)
Enable Campaign Budget Optimization (CBO)
Set daily budget: (Target CPA × 50 conversions) / 7 days minimum
Attribution Setting: 7-day click (default) or Incremental (advanced)

step.title: Ad Set Level Configuration
step.action: Targeting: Broad (Age 18-65+, All Genders, Geographic limit only)
Conversion Event: Purchase (Ecom) or Lead/Qualified Lead (Lead Gen)
If lead gen with above 7 day lag time: Use mid-funnel event (Booked Call/Qualified Lead)
Placements: Advantage+ Placements (Automatic)

step.title: Exclusions (Critical for M3 Swim Lane Integrity)
step.action: Prospecting: Exclude 180-day Purchasers
Prospecting: Exclude 30-day Site Visitors (optional, stricter prospecting)
Validate: Use Audience Overlap tool to confirm under 5% overlap with Retargeting audiences

step.title: Creative Upload
step.action: Ensure 3-5 distinct creative formats (not variations)
Formats: Mix of video + static OR all video OR all static (don't mix in Flexible Ads)
Use 3:2:2 structure (3 creatives, 2 headlines, 2 texts) OR 5x5 for high-volume tests

step.title: Ad Level Settings (Critical)
step.action: Turn OFF "Visual Touch-ups" (Meta auto-cropping destroys brand assets)
Turn OFF "Advantage+ Creative" unless you want Meta altering your copy
Review: Preview ad on mobile (where 80% of users see it)

step.title: Final Pre-Launch Check
step.action: Pixel: Verify Events Manager shows recent Purchase/Lead events (last 24h)
Budget: Confirm sufficient for 50 conversions in 7 days
Exclusions: Double-check custom audiences refreshing daily
Launch

quality_gates.gate: Budget must be sufficient for learning phase: (CPA × 50) / 7 minimum
Exclusions validated via Audience Overlap tool (under 5% overlap)
Pixel showing activity in last 24 hours (not dormant)
Creatives are concepts (different hooks/stories), not variations (color swaps)

sop.name: Nano Banana AI Asset Creation
sop.purpose: Use GenAI (Gemini Nano Banana Pro) to rapidly generate variations of winning static ads by swapping models, backgrounds, or products while maintaining composition.

step.title: Identify Winning Static Ad
step.action: Filter ads by format: Static Image
Find winner: Low CPA + High Spend (>$1k total)
Download image file from Ads Manager

step.title: Upload to Gemini (Nano Banana Pro Model)
step.action: Open Gemini chat interface
Upload winning ad image
Also upload: Product image (if swapping product) or lifestyle image (if swapping background)

step.title: Prompt for Variation
step.action: Prompt structure: "Replace [Element A] with [Element B], keep composition identical"
Example 1: "Replace the t-shirt the man is wearing with the windbreaker from image 2. Keep pose and background identical."
Example 2: "Change the background from kitchen to outdoor park, keep model and product placement identical."
Example 3: "Replace the woman in image 1 with a man of similar age, keep all other elements identical."

step.title: Generate Multiple Variations
step.action: Generate 4-10 variations using different prompts
Variations to test: Different models (age/gender/ethnicity), different backgrounds, different times of day
Export all as PNG/JPG

step.title: Launch as New Pack
step.action: Upload all variations to Prospecting CBO as new Ad Set "Pack [Date] - Nano Banana"
Monitor for 7 days
Expectation: Extends lifespan of winning concept by 2-4 weeks

quality_gates.gate: Original winning ad must have >$1k spend + profitable CPA (proven winner)
Variations must maintain composition (same layout, just swapped elements)
Generate minimum 5 variations (need volume for testing)

sop.name: Ad Set Spending Limit Injection
sop.frequency: When launching new tests in established CBOs
sop.purpose: Force algorithm to allocate budget to new creative packs that would otherwise receive zero spend due to CBO favoring historical winners.

step.title: Identify Starved Ad Set
step.action: Review CBO spend distribution over last 3 days
Find new ad set receiving $0 or under 5% of budget

step.title: Calculate Minimum Spend
step.action: Determine Target CPA (e.g., $50)
Set Daily Minimum = 1x Target CPA ($50/day)

step.title: Apply Spending Limit
step.action: Go to starved Ad Set, then Budget and Schedule
Enable "Ad set spending limit"
Enter Daily Minimum: $50

step.title: Set Calendar Reminder
step.action: Mark calendar for Day 7 review

step.title: Day 7 Decision
step.action: IF CPA below Target AND spend exceeded minimum , then Remove limit (it's a winner)
IF CPA above Target AND spend stayed at minimum , then Pause ad set (it failed)

quality_gates.gate: Only apply to ad sets getting $0 spend for 3+ days (confirmed starvation)
Remove limit by Day 7 maximum (don't leave on indefinitely)
Maximum 1-2 ad sets with limits simultaneously in one CBO (prevent fragmentation)

sop.name: Post-Andromeda Optimization Routine
sop.purpose: Light-touch optimization focused on creative refresh and concept performance, not micro-management.

step.title: Check Ad Set Structure
step.action: IF spread across above 5 tiny ad sets , then Consolidate to 1-2 Broad CBOs

step.title: Check Ad Frequency
step.action: IF Frequency above 3.0 in Prospecting , then Creative fatigued, refresh needed

step.title: Check Concept Performance (Not Just Individual Ads)
step.action: Group ads by concept type
Identify which concepts are winning (UGC vs Founder vs Demo)

step.title: Kill Losers
step.action: IF ad has high CPA (>2x target) AND low spend (under $100 total after 7 days) , then Pause
Do NOT pause ads with high spend (let CBO starve them naturally)

quality_gates.gate: Only pause ads that are both high CPA AND low spend (clear losers)
Frequency above 3.0 is the refresh trigger, not subjective "feeling"

category.name: Diagnostic
category.heuristic: IF learning limited above 7 days THEN consolidate ad sets or increase budget to hit 50 conversions/week [SRC: Ben Heath | 580]
IF retargeting ROAS is high (>4x) but blended ROAS is low THEN you are over-investing in retargeting; shift budget to prospecting [SRC: Sam Piliero | 511]
IF one ad hogs 90% of CBO spend THEN launch a separate "Testing Campaign" to force budget to new concepts [SRC: Ben Heath | 578]
IF CPMs are spiking THEN check creative diversity; Andromeda penalizes repetitive formats [SRC: THE ECOM KING | 372]
IF "One Day View" conversions are above 50% of total THEN your ads are retargeting, not prospecting; switch to Incremental/Click attribution [SRC: Tier 11 | 627]
IF CPA rises but CTR is stable THEN the issue is likely post-click (Landing Page/Offer) [SRC: Chase Chappell | 250]
IF frequency above 3.0 in prospecting THEN creative is fatigued; launch new concept immediately [SRC: The Ecom King | 391]

category.name: Optimization
category.heuristic: IF spending under $3k/month THEN use 1 Campaign (Prospecting) + 1 Retargeting (Retention) [SRC: Sam Piliero | 644]
IF spending >$30k/month THEN use Cost Caps to manage volatility [SRC: Sam Piliero | 653]
IF an ad has high Incremental ROAS (lift) THEN graduate it to the Scaling/Control ad set [SRC: Sam Piliero | 509]
IF launching a new CBO test THEN use Ad Set Spending Limits (Min = 1x CPA) for the first 7 days [SRC: Sam Piliero | 654]
IF under $1M annual revenue THEN focus on ONE avatar with multiple angles, not multiple avatars [SRC: Dr. Matt Shiver | 54]
IF broad targeting fails THEN test "Interest Clusters" as a temporary guide for the pixel [SRC: Sam Piliero | 641]
IF exclusion logic is working THEN Audience Overlap tool should show under 5% overlap between Prospecting and Retargeting [SRC: Sam Piliero | 510]

category.name: Scaling
category.heuristic: IF a creative works THEN do not pause it; create a new "Pack" with variations to run alongside it [SRC: Precision Frameworks | 608]
IF you spend $10k/month THEN produce at least 1 new ad per week (Rule of 1:10k) [SRC: Sam Piliero | 190]
IF scaling a winner THEN use "Post ID Harvesting" to move it to the control campaign without losing engagement [SRC: Professor Charley T | 103]
IF ncROAS above 1.5x THEN you have permission to scale budget by 20% [SRC: Justin Lalonde | 7]
IF spending >$10k/mo THEN ensure you are launching at least 1 new creative concept per week [SRC: Sam Piliero | 190]

category.name: Troubleshooting
category.heuristic: IF lead gen lag time is above 7 days THEN optimize for "Qualified Lead" or "Call Booked" instead of "Sale" [SRC: Caden Thompson | 290]
IF static ads are failing THEN test "Native" formats (Apple Notes, News Articles) [SRC: Omar Eddaoudi | 268]
IF clients demand "On-Brand" creatives but "Ugly" ads perform better THEN run both in separate ad sets to prove the data [SRC: Justin Lalonde | 10]
IF using Cost Caps and spend stops THEN raise bid by 10-20% to re-enter auction [SRC: Sam Piliero | 521]
IF new pack gets zero spend in CBO THEN it is not necessarily a failure; use Ad Set Limits to force a test [SRC: Sam Piliero | 770]

metric.name: ncROAS (New Customer ROAS)
metric.action_trigger: IF below1.0x for 7 days , then Stop scaling

metric.name: Incremental ROAS
metric.action_trigger: IF below0.5 , then Ad is retargeting, not prospecting

metric.name: Hook Rate
metric.action_trigger: IF below25% , then Fix first 3 seconds

metric.name: Hold Rate
metric.action_trigger: IF high hook but low hold , then Clickbait issue

metric.name: Conversion Volume
metric.action_trigger: IF below50 , then Consolidate ad sets

metric.name: Lag Time
metric.action_trigger: IF above 7 days , then Move optimization up-funnel

metric.name: MER (Marketing Efficiency Ratio)
metric.action_trigger: IF Platform ROAS rises but MER flat , then Attribution hallucination

metric.name: Prospecting Spend %
metric.action_trigger: IF below50% , then Over-investing in Retargeting

metric.name: Retargeting Spend %
metric.action_trigger: IF above 40% , then Reduce Retargeting budget

metric.name: Retention Spend %
metric.action_trigger: IF above 15% , then Inflating acquisition metrics

metric.name: Event Match Quality
metric.action_trigger: IF below6.0 , then Fix CAPI/Pixel setup

metric.name: CPM (Cost Per 1000 Impressions)
metric.action_trigger: IF doubles week-over-week , then Creative fatigue or quality drop

patterns.pattern: Format: [Funnel]_[Objective]_[Concept]_[Date] Example: PROS_CONV_UGC-Mashup_2026-01-19 Purpose: Enables fast sorting, filtering, and performance tracking by concept
Order: Prospecting First , then Validate ncROAS , then Add Retargeting , then Add Retention Rationale: Must establish new customer profitability before layering in warm/existing customer campaigns

Prospecting excludes (Retargeting + Retention audiences) Retargeting excludes (Retention audience only) Retention includes (180-day Purchasers) with no exclusions Purpose: Prevents audience overlap and ROAS inflation
Grouping 3-6 creatives into a single ad set ("Pack") rather than 1 ad per ad set Benefits: Data consolidation, faster learning phase, organized tracking
Remove all interests, lookalikes, and demographics (except Age 18-65+ and Geographic limits) Rationale: Andromeda algorithm performs best with broad targeting; creative does the targeting
Using 3 Creatives × 2 Headlines × 2 Texts to consolidate learning data Alternative: 5x5 for high-volume testing (5 × 5 × 5)
Switch from "Highest Volume" to "Cost per Result Goal" at $30k+/month spend Mechanism: Spend becomes variable (low on bad days, high on good days), but CPA stays flat
First 3 seconds must have: High contrast, weird textures, or "scam" warnings to stop scroll Examples: Weird rocks (curiosity), color blocking (contrast), native formats (pattern interrupt)
Face-to-camera storytelling from the owner/founder builds authority and trust Use case: New brands, high-ticket offers, competitive markets
Ads that look like Notes app, Twitter screenshots, or News articles outperform polished commercial content Rationale: Bypasses "ad blindness" by blending into organic content feed

checklist.name: Campaign Launch Checklist
checklist.item: [ ] Campaign Objective = Sales (Ecom) or Leads (Lead Gen) [SRC: Jared Robinson | 81]
[ ] CBO Enabled [SRC: Sam Piliero | 508]
[ ] Daily Budget = (Target CPA × 50 conversions) / 7 days minimum [SRC: Ben Heath | 580]
[ ] Attribution = 7-day click (default) or Incremental (advanced) [SRC: Sam Piliero | 716]
[ ] Exclusions set: 180-day Purchasers excluded from Prospecting [SRC: Jared Robinson | 81]
[ ] Audience Overlap validated (under 5% overlap between lanes) [SRC: Sam Piliero | 510]
[ ] Targeting = Broad (Age 18-65+, All Genders, Geo only) [SRC: Dr. Matt Shiver | 59]
[ ] Placements = Advantage+ (Automatic) [SRC: Chase Chappell | 729]
[ ] "Visual Touch-ups" turned OFF [SRC: Chase Chappell | 730]
[ ] 3-5 Distinct Creatives uploaded (concepts, not variations) [SRC: Chase Chappell | 728]
[ ] Pixel verified active (Events Manager shows activity last 24h) [SRC: Tier 11 | 699]
[ ] For Lead Gen: Optimization event matches lag time (under 7 days = Purchase, >7 days = Qualified Lead) [SRC: Caden Thompson | 290]

checklist.name: Weekly Performance Review
checklist.item: [ ] Check ncROAS (New Customer Revenue / Ad Spend) [SRC: Sam Piliero | 777]
[ ] Check MER (Total Revenue / Total Ad Spend all channels) [SRC: Sam Piliero | 777]
[ ] Compare Incremental vs Platform ROAS (validate attribution accuracy) [SRC: Sam Piliero | 595]
[ ] Harvest winning Post IDs to Control/Scale campaign [SRC: Professor Charley T | 103]
[ ] Check Frequency across Prospecting ads (>3.0 = fatigue danger) [SRC: THE ECOM KING | 391]
[ ] Verify Prospecting getting 60-70% of total spend [SRC: Sam Piliero | 511]
[ ] Brief new creative pack using Creative Flywheel SOP (4-6 iterations + 1 new concept) [SRC: Sam Piliero | 656]
[ ] Check Event Match Quality score in Events Manager (>7.0 target) [SRC: Tier 11 | 699]
[ ] Review Pack System: Any packs getting $0 spend for 5+ days? (Pause them) [SRC: Sam Piliero | 508]
[ ] Review ad sets with Spending Limits: Day 7 arrived? (Remove or pause per performance) [SRC: Sam Piliero | 654]

compliance_guardrails.guardrail: Broad targeting is now required for efficient AI delivery; restrictive targeting increases CPM and fights Andromeda optimization [SRC: THE ECOM KING | 373]
MUST exclude 180-day purchasers from Prospecting to ensure accurate ncROAS measurement [SRC: Jared Robinson | 81]
Creative diversity is mandatory: Andromeda penalizes repetitive formats; test concepts (different stories), not variations (color swaps) [SRC: Alex Robinson | 121]
Budget scaling must be gradual: Maximum 20% increase every 2-3 days to avoid resetting learning phase [SRC: AI Strategist | 46]
Custom audiences (exclusions) must auto-refresh daily; stale audiences cause overlap failures [SRC: Sam Piliero | 510]
Do NOT turn off "Visual Touch-ups" option can crop/alter brand assets unpredictably [SRC: Chase Chappell | 730]
Minimum conversion volume: 50 conversions/week per ad set for stable optimization [SRC: Ben Heath | 580]
For Lead Gen: If lag time above 7 days, optimize for mid-funnel events (Qualified Lead, Booked Call) not Purchase [SRC: Caden Thompson | 290]
